items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You've come, warrior!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The mighty guardian, our Iudicis Arsinosa, is not the kind of Echo to warm to crowds.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Lioness favors the strong, the unyielding, and the battle-hardened. Only those she deems worthy will find her arms welcoming.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But... she is still an Echo and can't talk.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (These sounds...)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: What is it? Did you feel something just now?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I only heard strange sounds. Tell me, what direction did the Lioness just look toward?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The <te href=850160>Vale of Glory</te>.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It's where all heroes, blessed by the glory forged in iron and fire, find their rest.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But on the long path of hunting, many died with their bodies missing. Only their weapons returned.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Arsinosa's frequency is woven from the cries of souls in battle. The battered weapons still carry the will of their former masters, whispering the deeds left undone.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: To touch such weapons is a rite of great importance for those who claim glory in the Great Agon and receive the Lioness's blessing.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Hearing the voices of our ancestors by Arsinosa's side is a great source of strength for Septimontians.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Before meeting Buling, when I touched the Echo in that village, I felt... a layered, indistinct frequency.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (It was like what Abby described on our journey to Septimont, but the source eluded me at the time.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But now... I've found it.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The source of that power lies in the Vale of Glory. The closer the Lioness gets to it, the clearer it becomes.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: She waits for us there. Shall we go together?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I'm not sure if Septimontian rituals will interest you, but you might uncover something useful, no?

options.text: I'm happy to go with my fellow warrior.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Perfect! Let's head out.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ugh...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What is it?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Nothing... just...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...Just a dizzy spell. Overexcitement after the win... or something.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Anyway, it's no matter. Let's continue.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: You... are...

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: From my homeland...

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: Septimont...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That girl just now... that was Mya.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The closer to the Vale of Glory, the more the normal world and the anomaly <color=RedD>overlap</color>. Whatever lies ahead must be the key to all of this.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I need to rethink everything. Septimont, Lupa, Cartethyia, Mya, the Lioness of Glory... all of it.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Cartethyia arrived in Septimont alone, not waiting for me or attempting contact. Something must have happened to prevent her.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If something cut her off, aside from the Fractsidus, it could've only been the Threnodian, which should be dead.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But Septimont holds no faith in the Sentinel, and the Threnodian was never able to establish influence here. Hmm... maybe it's not that simple.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The Threnodian controls the minds of Its followers through their faith. Mya is a Ragunnesi, and likely a follower of The Order of the Deep.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If the Vale of Glory is an important rite for champions, perhaps Mya went there... and it was there she vanished.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Her hysterics, hallucinations, and loss of control in the arena were signs of the Threnodian's corruption.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Phrolova once said, "The closer one stands to the <color=RedD>protagonist</color>, the greater their influence over the tale."

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (In this Great Agon, Lupa is the <color=RedD>protagonist</color>. Years ago, it was Mya. Now, I'm seeing Mya's image flicker in and out throughout this journey.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If the Mya I see is under the Threnodian's control, does that mean securing the <color=RedD>protagonist</color> role is key to Its plan?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (When I first met Lupa, I sensed what felt like Cartethyia's frequency on her. Later, I also felt the Threnodian's presence.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Did they cross paths? If Cartethyia is part of this story, the likelihood is high.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Could Lupa have forgotten? Did they meet without realizing it? Or is she lying and already under the Threnodian's influence?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Was Phrolova telling the truth by saying Lupa would bring forth the Dark Tide... or is she just another one of its victims?)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Hmm? Hey, is something wrong?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Is there something you need? Or... something you want to say?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I'm sorry. I don't recall crossing paths with Cartethyia. But... you could be right. I may have, and just forgotten about it.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: These past days, there have been moments when... I feel lost, forgetting what I was doing a heartbeat ago.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't know if it's the Threnodian's work, but... if something is wrong, and it's trying to twist my mind...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You'd make the right call. Wouldn't you, my fellow warrior?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You'd protect my honor and my dignity.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: There's more at stake than that, Lupa.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I've heard the Septimont saying, "All for glory, fear no death." But sometimes, living takes more courage than dying.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Heh, well then, that'll do just fine!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I trust you, warrior, just as you trust me.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It's okay, don't worry about it.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...Is that so?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't know what's weighing on your mind, but you accepted my invitation, and we fought as one in the arena.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: As a warrior, I trust the clash of steel far more than words!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The traces of the blade reflect the soul of its wielder.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Alright! Don't wear that face. Whatever it is, I trust you'll make the right call.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Septimont is my city. Don't let it lose its glory or pride. Will you promise me that, warrior?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll do what I can.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (It doesn't matter if the <color=RedD>story's end</color> is already written, or yet to unfold...)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I'll trust my own judgment to guide it where it must go.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Tidal Blight... It's an outgrowth of the Dark Tide itself.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Why is it appearing suddenly like this...?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: We'll clear it out first, then check if that sword is the source.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: What's that sound... Come, we should check it out!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: This... doesn't feel like the first time I've been here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Let's go.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ha... huff... Ugh!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: A-a dream? No, it couldn't...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It's no dream.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I'm not alone. The Warrior put the hope of change in my hands.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Heh... This feels good. Not standing there alone on the champion's stage.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ({PlayerName} hasn't learned this yet, but I now remember traveling with Cartethyia. I can't be in the real world.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (This is a Sonoro created by the Fractsidus. It's a realm of <color=RedD>stories</color>.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (The story of the Great Agon lasts only three days. Before Cartethyia came here, I... had wandered this same bloody story more times than I can count.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (The Dark Tide consumes Septimont, and that monstrous half-human, half-sea creature grows ever larger... Is this the Fractsidus plan? To feed it with Septimont's ruins?)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (A script... Yes, this Sonoro is like a book of plays.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Each world in the Sonoro is a story that keeps repeating, like sheets of paper, all <color=RedD>overlapping</color> with one another.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (New pages keep appearing, layering over the old, and the changes in one story bleed through to the next.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (After Cartethyia's actions, Septimont, once destroyed by the Dark Tide, has begun to return to normal in the story where {PlayerName} arrived.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Cartethyia saved me, changed my fate, and led me to the Warrior, who stopped the Threnodian in the last story. So in this time, I haven't lost my memory.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ({PlayerName} had delivered a powerful blow against the monster, and the energy supply from the Central Hub has been cut off.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (But that's not even its full form...)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (I once searched for its true form with Cartethyia. I remember now, what we seek lies in the lower city's waters.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I won't let your trust be misplaced, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: Still lingering, Mya?

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: A fading Reverberation that should have dissolved in the deep long ago.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: ...Where... are you going...?

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: To claim the title.

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: Was that not your unfulfilled wish?

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: That... was never... mine... the arena...

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: I... don't... need... this... victory...!

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: But I do. Without a champion and the Lion Beast, I cannot touch the Central Hub left by Imperator.

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: You... Cartethyia, both my "daughters," my very creations! Why this fruitless resistance?

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: Has Septimont ever granted you kindness?

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: They neither love you nor need you. Long have you been forgotten.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: We were... raised... by... them...

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: You... will never... understand...

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: Proud in battle, and yet yearning for strength. I know the heart of the Septimontians far better than you.

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: Mya, you are simply too afraid to face their true nature. You choose to deceive yourself.

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: You cannot admit that they used you and then cast you aside. You cannot accept that, from the start, you were insignificant.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: No... Viburnum... my... flowers...

items.speaker: Lady of the Sea
items.text: Hush. It matters not. Your thoughts are of little consequence.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (I've always carried this shame.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Shame for something I didn't choose, shame for something that's just there. Sometimes, it turns into doubt.）

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (I try to live free! And I do, most days. But still...  I see it. I see the pain in their faces.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (In the ones who fall behind. The ones who leave the field beaten. The ones who pin all their hopes on me...)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (I've always wondered, if I didn't have this gift, could I... could I've reached this point with clean hands?)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Should I have judged those who trample the rules to survive? Was I blind to my own arrogance?)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Now... I don't think like that anymore.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: {PlayerName} is stronger than me, no doubt, but {Male=he;Female=she} treats everyone the same. {Male=He;Female=She} didn't choose to stand alone. Instead, {Male=he;Female=she} chose to trust me.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Cartethyia spent years trapped by the weight of everyone's expectations. Never stepped foot in Septimont before, but still sailed across the seas to be here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: And Mya... Her Resonance ability might be weak, but she never ran from a fight. She fought 'til her last breath.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: A heart isn't shaped by what it's born with or what it's been given.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I've underestimated others... and I've underestimated myself, too.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But you! Arrogance! Thinking you understand the darkness in people's hearts. But you don't have a single drop of life in you.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Beautiful as ever, the <te href=850150>Gryphon's Fortress</te>. My city, Septimont.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Built by the hands of the people. Not the Sentinel, and not the Threnodian.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Where there's light, there's always shadow.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But people need light. They'll chase it no matter what. And they'll learn to live with the shadow.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Mya, Cartethyia… They were born and raised on these islands. They are daughters of Rinascita. They belong to no gods but their own!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It's real… Both of you are safe!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Threnodian's frequency is gone. Well done, Lupa.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You've found {PlayerName} and safeguarded Septimont... It's like something out of a knight's tale. Hmm, no, wait... Gladiator's tale?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Phew! Finally, I can stick my head out! {PlayerName}, are you okay?!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'm fine, Abby. What happened to you earlier?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I… wait, we're already in Septimont?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I was just talking to you, and then boom! It felt like something just... covered me!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: First, it messed with my frequency, then... I just passed out. Wait! Cartethyia? And a girl I don't know?!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Heh, you're lively. Your Echo's more boisterous than a Ta-da!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: W-what happened while I was out?!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Hmm... I think it's time we compare notes. Let's fill in this <color=RedD>story's</color> blanks, shall we?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It's Mya's sword. I went through a lot to bring it back.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The monster is gone now. Like so many Septimontian heroes whose bodies were never recovered, I... wanted to return her weapon.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: This is where she gave her life. I can't say if she loved or hated Septimont more.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But... she once spoke of the Bloodleaf Viburnum. It's a tribute often given to Gladiators by their supporters.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: So, I think... she would have wanted to rest here.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It's beautiful...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Our city, the City of Glory. The people here love to drink, laugh, carve statues, and hunt.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: It burns like fire, drenched in both victory and defeat. It holds all our disappointments and all our hopes.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: When Lupa vanished, I destroyed the experimental unit, pursued the Threnodian, and defeated the Lioness of Glory It controlled. Then I came across this place.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You remember how the Threnodian took on a part of the Sentinel's knowledge and permissions, yes?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: For the Sentinel, the information stolen was Their very memories.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: For this reason, even the Sentinel forgot that before merging with the Threnodian, They transferred some of their knowledge to the Central Hub in Septimont.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The knowledge They would later forget...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It was a secret, one the Threnodian should never learn. The Sentinel moved it there for safekeeping.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Within it, I found a special frequency. A part of it... felt like yours. I believe it's what the Sentinel left for you. So I took it.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: You feel a faint frequency of sharp, cold wind. It fades quickly as it touches your hand, but not before it points towards a distant destination in Septimont.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It seems like a clue... the true secret They left for you is not here but in that direction.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You see, before I came to meet you, I passed by the city of Septimont.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The arena I destroyed has been restored, but in the people's hearts, it's still {PlayerName} and Lupa who claimed victory.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Fractsidus's power to reshape reality is deeper than we realized. I'm afraid this is just the beginning.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I once believed the Threnodian sought to use the Fractsidus to regain power, but Its true goal is the Central Hub. I believe it's due to this secret the Sentinel has hidden.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That direction... It leads to the hunting grounds.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The <te href=850171>Blightcloud</te> still lingers there. When the <te href=850159>High Tide</te> comes, Septimontians hunt and cull the spawning Tacet Discords.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Dark Tide... connects everything.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I cannot pinpoint where the Sentinel's message lies. I'm afraid you must go there yourself, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But the worst of the storm has passed. Let's take a moment to rest.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Lupa, will you be our tour guide? Show us the sights and glories of Septimont?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'd love to hear more about what you mentioned earlier. The wind blowing through golden flags and the sound of wind chimes...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Of course! It would be my honor.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But before we go, there's something I need to do first.
Thou...
Thou carry the scent of Cartethyia.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Not just knowledge...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You've stolen the Sentinel's access too!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa's <color=RedD>role</color> in this story...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Isn't yours!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Is that... Contender Mya?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: It's the Threnodian.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa, this is something only you can do.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Tell me what I need to do.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Read the script. Adapt as required.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I believe in you, warrior.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (In a past <color=RedD>story</color>, I came here with Cartethyia...)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (I remember the entrance to the underground waterway is in the Old Districts Bell Tower.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Ah... Of course! The upkeep of these waters always falls under the Silvas' jurisdiction.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Maybe I can awaken this sleeping Giant of Glory.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (In Septimontian tradition, when it bears its blade and watches over the warriors leaving for the hunt, all gates and passages open.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Many of the old routes are blocked, but I can try draining the water through the remaining ones. It's worth a try!)

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Look at you... you can't go on much longer.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: I feared as much. Then this is where we... stop.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Strange. I remember everything about Septimont, but... I can't remember... I can't even remember my name.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You didn't forget.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Someone stole your <color=RedD>role</color> in the story. Even your name on the placards in the arena would be unreadable.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Your existence has been erased. Only by unmaking this story can it be reclaimed.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Too bad I won't last long enough to see that happen.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: I'm sorry, Cartethyia. You saved me, but I can't help you in return.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: When I was a child... long ago, I heard tales of Septimont.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: A city full of passion and life. People there drank deeply, laughed loudly, appreciated sculpture, and lived for the hunt.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Men and women feared nothing. They burned like fire, giving all their light and heat before fading.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: But when I arrived in Septimont, it had already been undone by dark hands. With the Dark Tide sweeping the streets, the city was nothing but an empty shell.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: That's right... my city is beautiful.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: When the autumn wind blew through the golden flags and wind chimes, time seemed to slow...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Leviathan... I will not give It the chance to make the people of Rinascita weep and bleed.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I will drag whoever orchestrated this tragedy out of the shadows.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Septimont... can I leave it to you?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: No.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You said it yourself. This is your city. Some things, only you can do.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The monster leaving its trace in the Dark Tide... I can feel it. Its frequency is similar to my own.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Threnodian created me to be a Resonator for Its power. But I wasn't the only one. Many "vessels" before me failed.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: You mean...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: There is still much I don't know. I'll keep searching. But no matter what, you have a certain "specialness."

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Once... I sought to end it all. Someone stopped me.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Even a life mocked by fate can break its curse. Whatever you seek to do, whether you're weak or strong, you must strive for it.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I returned to the world thanks to a certain person's help.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I believe {Male=he;Female=she} will come here and reshape this twisted story.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Must be quite the person, eh? I wish I could meet {Male=him;Female=her}... and see how {Male=he;Female=she} does it...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Then you will see for yourself.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Victory isn't won in an instant. It takes patience and perseverance. What we can't do now, someone will carry forward later.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: If you want to save your city, then hold on. No matter the pain, no matter the doubts, you must keep going.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Just like how you wandered Septimont before I came, broken yet determined to leave to find help for the city.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Jump to a new story, and bring my message to that person.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Alright, Cartethyia... I'll... I'll try my best...

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: First... tell me, what is that person's name?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I'm close to that abnormal frequency...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: ...You're in the submerged aqueducts.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The submerged aqueducts... I remember reading about them in the Silva records. There used to be an underground waterway leading to the plains.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But now, with the Old District completely flooded, we can only access it if we drain the water first.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (After my role was erased, Cartethyia must've found the half-human, half-sea creature... and the Fractsidus.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Like {PlayerName}, she chose to remain on the closing of that page. To give new stories a fighting chance.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (That's why... I only regained my name after I met the Warrior.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (During our journey, the overlapping worlds the Warrior saw were the monster's doing. It tried to use Mya to steal my role again... to corrupt Septimont once more.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: (Only I can erase it from this story. Only I can prove that I'm real, and it is fake.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...What happened to the Mya who vanished in the Vale of Glory decades ago?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: After everything you went through, what did the city of Septimont mean to you...
